hero.question: Will Israel strike 3 countries in 2026?
hero.prob_take: At 7.2%, the market is pricing Israel striking 3 separate countries in 2026 as a tail risk event, essentially ruling it out as a base case. The real question is whether regional escalation (Iran, Syria, Lebanon, Hezbollah proxies) unfolds beyond the current conflict arc, and 7.2% probably underweights the tail if tensions spike post-ceasefire or if Trump tilts regional calculations toward Iranian confrontation.

daily_take.headline: Israel's Three-Country Strike Bet Just Got Cheaper. Smart Money Disagrees.
daily_take.deck: The "Israel strikes 3 countries in 2026" market collapsed 6.5 points to 7.2% overnight on $647K volume. Traders are pricing in a cooling conflict, but the geopolitical infrastructure for escalation remains intact. At these odds, you're getting paid 13-to-1 for a scenario that's far from priced out. The play: nibble longs on any further dips below 7%.

sidebar.headline: Iran entry odds barely moved despite 10.5% pricing, but $29.6M in volume suggests institutional conviction the threshold stays above 10% all year.
sidebar.label: US forces enter Iran: 10.5%

sidebar.headline: Arizona's tournament odds just jumped 2.2 points on recent tourney performance. Sharps are already in. Late money is chasing.
sidebar.label: Arizona NCAA 2026: 28.0%

sidebar.headline: DHS funding bet ticked up 2.5 points to 20% as April 15 deadline approaches. Thin volume signals this could gap on actual legislative movement.
sidebar.label: DHS funding by Apr 15: 20.0%
